title: Contributing
text: Thank interest contributing pkgmkr. guide describes expected workflow reporting issues submitting changes R package.

title: Contributing
text: opening issue, please check whether behavior already documented reported issue tracker. useful bug report include: operating system R version, sessionInfo(). installed pkgmkr version. smallest reproducible example triggers problem. full error, warning, unexpected output. relevant package-development context, whether working RStudio, temporary directory, existing package project. Small reproducible examples especially valuable pkgmkr creates package structures disk. Please use tempdir() another disposable path possible.

title: Contributing
text: Clone repository, install package dependencies R: Install local package development mode : pak available, devtools::install() also fine.

title: Contributing
text: Please keep changes focused. pull request fixes one behavior, updates matching tests, refreshes relevant documentation easier review one mixes unrelated cleanup functional changes. exported functions: Update roxygen comments R/. Run roxygen2::roxygenise() refresh NAMESPACE files man/. Add update examples can run safely temporary directory. Add focused tests tests/testthat/. changing behavior creates files directories, prefer tests use withr::local_tempdir(). keeps checks isolated avoids leaving package artifacts contributor’s working directory.

title: Contributing
text: submitting pull request, run: package pass R CMD check without errors, warnings, unexpected notes. note unavoidable, describe pull request.

title: Contributing
text: Documentation changes made source files possible: Function documentation belongs roxygen comments R/. Package articles belong vignettes/ pkgdown article directory already used project. User-facing changes reflected README.md appropriate. Release-facing changes noted NEWS.md.

title: Contributing
text: Open pull request topic branch include: short summary change. issue number, pull request fixes existing issue. checks ran. intentional limitations follow-work. contributing, agree contributions licensed license pkgmkr.

title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: pkgmkr helps create new R package without setup hand every time. can used quick one-time setup config file reuse future packages.

what: Create a package with one function call
title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: Start mk_pkg() want new package main setup files. creates package called mypackage current working directory.
code: library(pkgmkr)  mk_pkg(   path = "mypackage",   author = "Jane Doe",   email = "jane.doe@example.com",   git = FALSE,   check_pkg_name = FALSE,   pkgdown = FALSE )

previous_headings: Start here > Create a package with one function call
title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: path sets package folder name full path. author author name. must include first last name. email optional. include , added package metadata. git controls whether Git repository initialized. check_pkg_name controls whether pkgmkr checks package name available using available package. pkgdown controls whether pkgdown site set built.

what: What gets created when you run mk_pkg()
title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: pkgmkr handles basic setup can start writing code sooner. successful run, package include main files usually make hand: DESCRIPTION package metadata NAMESPACE exported functions R/ package code man/ generated documentation license files based selected license README.md readme_md = TRUE Git setup git = TRUE pkgdown setup pkgdown = TRUE

what: Use options when you need more control
title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: need every argument first time. Start small, add options need . example, want Git pkgdown start: Use already want Git pkgdown site.
code: mk_pkg(   path = "~/projects/myanalysispkg",   author = "Jane Doe",   email = "jane.doe@example.com",   git = TRUE,   git_username = "janedoe",   git_email = "jane.doe@example.com",   license = "GPL-3",   pkgdown = TRUE )

what: Create a reusable package template with a config file
title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: make packages often, keep one config file usual settings. can reuse instead typing values time. mk_pkg_from_config() expects YAML JSON file least required fields: pkg_name first_name last_name can also include optional fields like email, git, license, check_pkg_name, readme_md, pkgdown.

previous_headings: Start here > Create a reusable package template with a config file
title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: Save file standard-package.yml: gives reusable template. new packages, need change pkg_name.
code: pkg_name: mypackage first_name: Jane last_name: Doe email: jane.doe@example.com readme_md: true git: false check_pkg_name: true license: MIT pkgdown: false

previous_headings: Start here > Create a reusable package template with a config file
what: Create the package from the YAML file
title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: helps keep package setup consistent across projects. want generate YAML file R instead writing hand, use write_config().
code: mk_pkg_from_config("standard-package.yml", file_type = "yaml") standard_config <- list(   pkg_name = "mypackage",   first_name = "Jane",   last_name = "Doe",   email = "jane.doe@example.com",   readme_md = TRUE,   git = FALSE,   check_pkg_name = TRUE,   license = "MIT",   pkgdown = FALSE )  write_config("standard-package.yml", standard_config)

title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: pkgmkr checks common inputs creates package. helps catch problems early. Package names must start letter. Package names can contain letters, numbers, dots. Package names end dot. Author names must include least first last name. Email addresses must valid provided. Licenses must currently MIT GPL-3. target directory must already exist.

title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: package created, common next step : Add functions R/. Document roxygen comments. Run devtools::document(). Add tests tests/. Build refine pkgdown site enabled .

title: Get started with pkgmkr
text: Browse reference documentation details. See examples mk_pkg(). See configuration helpers config-file workflow.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Shaurita D. Hutchins. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hutchins S (2026). pkgmkr: Create R Package One Simple Function. R package version 0.2.0, https://shauritahutchins.com/pkgmkr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {pkgmkr: Create an R Package with One Simple Function},   author = {Shaurita D. Hutchins},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://shauritahutchins.com/pkgmkr/}, }

what: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: pkgmkr simplifies process creating R packages providing single, straightforward function. designed user-friendly handle various package configurations, including optional Git integration, license selection, .

title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: Easy--use function package creation Optional Git repository initialization Customizable package metadata License selection (MIT GPL-3) README pkgdown site generation Configuration file support (YAML/JSON) Comprehensive input validation error handling

title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: Install pkgmkr r-universe:

title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: Create new R package essential features:
code: library(pkgmkr)  # Create a simple package mk_pkg(   path = "mypackage",   author = "Jane Doe",   email = "jane.doe@example.com",   git = FALSE,   readme_md = TRUE,   check_pkg_name = TRUE,   license = "MIT",   pkgdown = FALSE )

what: Full Example with Git and GitHub
title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: Create package Git repository GitHub integration:
code: library(pkgmkr)  mk_pkg(   path = "~/projects/myawesomepackage",   author = "John Smith",   email = "john.smith@example.com",   git = TRUE,   git_username = "johnsmith",   git_email = "john.smith@example.com",   readme_md = TRUE,   check_pkg_name = TRUE,   license = "GPL-3",   pkgdown = TRUE )

title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: can also create packages YAML JSON configuration file:
code: library(pkgmkr)  # Create a configuration file config <- list(   pkg_name = "mypackage",   first_name = "Jane",   last_name = "Doe",   email = "jane.doe@example.com",   readme_md = TRUE,   git = FALSE,   check_pkg_name = TRUE,   license = "MIT",   pkgdown = FALSE )  # Write the configuration write_config("my_package_config.yml", config)  # Create package from configuration mk_pkg_from_config("my_package_config.yml", file_type = "yaml")

title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: path: Path package created (can just package name full path) [required] author: Full name package author (e.g., “Jane Doe”) [required] email: Email address author [optional] - provided, DESCRIPTION include maintainer email git: Logical; whether initialize Git repository (default: TRUE) git_username: Git username repository configuration git_email: Git email repository configuration readme_md: Logical; whether create README.md file (default: TRUE) check_pkg_name: Logical; whether check package name availability CRAN (default: TRUE) license: License type - either “MIT” “GPL-3” (default: “MIT”) pkgdown: Logical; whether set build pkgdown documentation site (default: TRUE)

title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: pkgmkr includes comprehensive input validation help avoid common mistakes: Package names must start letter contain letters, numbers, dots Email addresses must properly formatted Author names must include least first last name package directory must already exist License must either “MIT” “GPL-3” validation errors provide clear, helpful messages guide fixing issue.

title: pkgmkr: Create R Packages with One Simple Function
text: Contributions welcome! Please read CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines contribute project.

title: Import Package Configuration — import_config
text: Import package configuration data YAML JSON file.

title: Import Package Configuration — import_config
text: path character. Path configuration file. Must valid file path existing file. file_type character. file type - must either "yaml" "json". Default: "yaml".

title: Import Package Configuration — import_config
text: list containing configuration data file.

title: Import Package Configuration — import_config
text: function reads validates configuration file. file must exist contain valid YAML JSON. file malformed, helpful error message indicate parsing issue.

title: Import Package Configuration — import_config
code: # Write a temporary YAML config, then import it config_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".yml") writeLines("pkg_name: demopkg\nfirst_name: Jane\nlast_name: Doe", config_path) config <- import_config(config_path, "yaml") print(config$pkg_name) #> [1] "demopkg" unlink(config_path)

title: Make Package — mk_pkg
text: Create new R package specified parameters. function automates process creating R package structure, including DESCRIPTION file, README, license files, optionally sets Git, GitHub, pkgdown documentation.

title: Make Package — mk_pkg
code: mk_pkg(   path,   author,   email = NULL,   git = TRUE,   git_username = NULL,   git_email = NULL,   readme_md = TRUE,   check_pkg_name = TRUE,   license = "MIT",   pkgdown = TRUE )

title: Make Package — mk_pkg
text: path character. path package created. Can simple package name (creates current directory) full path. Package name must start letter contain letters, numbers, dots. author character. Full name package author (e.g., "Jane Doe"). Must include least first last name separated space. email character. Email address author. Optional, must valid email format provided. git logical. Whether initialize Git repository package. Default: TRUE. git_username character. username use Git configuration. Optional; used git = TRUE. git_email character. email use Git configuration. Optional; used git = TRUE. readme_md logical. Whether create README.md file. Default: TRUE. check_pkg_name logical. Whether check package name available CRAN using 'available' package. Default: TRUE. license character. license use package. Must either "MIT" "GPL-3". Default: "MIT". pkgdown logical. Whether set build pkgdown documentation site. Default: TRUE. Note: can take time build.

title: Make Package — mk_pkg
text: Invisibly returns list three elements: path: Full path created package package_name: Name package success: TRUE package created successfully

title: Make Package — mk_pkg
text: function includes comprehensive input validation ensure: Package names follow R conventions (start letter, contain letters/numbers/dots) Email addresses properly formatted Author names include least first last name target directory already exist operation fails (e.g., Git/GitHub setup), function continues warns rather failing completely, ensuring still get usable package structure.

title: Make Package — mk_pkg
code: # Create a simple package with MIT license pkg_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "mypackage") mk_pkg(   path = pkg_dir,   author = "Jane Doe",   email = "jane.doe@example.com",   git = FALSE,   check_pkg_name = FALSE,   pkgdown = FALSE ) #> Creating package structure... #> ✔ Creating /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage/. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> ✔ Creating R/. #> ✔ Writing DESCRIPTION. #> Package: mypackage #> Title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case) #> Version: 0.0.0.9000 #> Authors@R (parsed): #>     * First Last <first.last@example.com> [aut, cre] #> Description: What the package does (one paragraph). #> License: `use_mit_license()`, `use_gpl3_license()` or friends to #>     pick a license #> Encoding: UTF-8 #> Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) #> RoxygenNote: 7.0.0 #> ✔ Writing NAMESPACE. #> ✔ Writing mypackage.Rproj. #> ✔ Adding "^mypackage\\.Rproj$" to .Rbuildignore. #> ✔ Adding ".Rproj.user" to .gitignore. #> ✔ Adding "^\\.Rproj\\.user$" to .Rbuildignore. #> ✔ Setting active project to "<no active project>". #> ✔ Setting active project to "/home/runner/work/pkgmkr/pkgmkr". #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> Setting up DESCRIPTION file... #> Creating README.md... #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> ✔ Writing README.md. #> ☐ Update README.md to include installation instructions. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> Setting up MIT license... #> ✔ Adding "MIT + file LICENSE" to License. #> ✔ Writing LICENSE. #> ✔ Writing LICENSE.md. #> ✔ Adding "^LICENSE\\.md$" to .Rbuildignore. #>  #> ============================================================ #> SUCCESS! Package 'mypackage' created at: #> /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage #> ============================================================ #> ✔ Setting active project to "/home/runner/work/pkgmkr/pkgmkr". unlink(pkg_dir, recursive = TRUE)  # Create a package with GPL-3 license pkg_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "analysisPkg") mk_pkg(   path = pkg_dir,   author = "John Smith Wilson",   email = "john@example.com",   git = FALSE,   check_pkg_name = FALSE,   license = "GPL-3",   pkgdown = FALSE ) #> Creating package structure... #> ✔ Creating /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/analysisPkg/. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/analysisPkg". #> ✔ Creating R/. #> ✔ Writing DESCRIPTION. #> Package: analysisPkg #> Title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case) #> Version: 0.0.0.9000 #> Authors@R (parsed): #>     * First Last <first.last@example.com> [aut, cre] #> Description: What the package does (one paragraph). #> License: `use_mit_license()`, `use_gpl3_license()` or friends to #>     pick a license #> Encoding: UTF-8 #> Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) #> RoxygenNote: 7.0.0 #> ✔ Writing NAMESPACE. #> ✔ Writing analysisPkg.Rproj. #> ✔ Adding "^analysisPkg\\.Rproj$" to .Rbuildignore. #> ✔ Adding ".Rproj.user" to .gitignore. #> ✔ Adding "^\\.Rproj\\.user$" to .Rbuildignore. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/home/runner/work/pkgmkr/pkgmkr". #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/analysisPkg". #> Setting up DESCRIPTION file... #> Creating README.md... #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/analysisPkg". #> ✔ Writing README.md. #> ☐ Update README.md to include installation instructions. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/analysisPkg". #> Setting up GPL-3 license... #> ✔ Adding "GPL (>= 3)" to License. #> ✔ Writing LICENSE.md. #> ✔ Adding "^LICENSE\\.md$" to .Rbuildignore. #>  #> ============================================================ #> SUCCESS! Package 'analysisPkg' created at: #> /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/analysisPkg #> ============================================================ #> ✔ Setting active project to "/home/runner/work/pkgmkr/pkgmkr". unlink(pkg_dir, recursive = TRUE)

title: Make Package from Config — mk_pkg_from_config
text: Create package based YAML JSON configuration file. provides convenient way store reuse package creation settings.

title: Make Package from Config — mk_pkg_from_config
text: config_path character. Path configuration file. File must exist. file_type character. file type configuration file. Must either "yaml" "json". Default: "yaml".

title: Make Package from Config — mk_pkg_from_config
text: Invisibly returns list package information (see mk_pkg).

title: Make Package from Config — mk_pkg_from_config
text: configuration file must contain minimum: pkg_name: Package name first_name: Author's first name last_name: Author's last name Optional fields include: email, readme_md, git, git_username, git_email, check_pkg_name, license, pkgdown.

title: Make Package from Config — mk_pkg_from_config
code: # Create a config file config_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pkg_config.yml") config <- list(   pkg_name = "mypackage",   first_name = "Jane",   last_name = "Doe",   email = "jane@example.com",   license = "MIT",   git = FALSE,   check_pkg_name = FALSE,   pkgdown = FALSE ) write_config(config_path, config) #> Configuration written successfully to: /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/pkg_config.yml  # Create package from config withr::with_dir(tempdir(), mk_pkg_from_config(config_path, file_type = "yaml")) #> Creating package from config file: /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/pkg_config.yml #> Creating package structure... #> ✔ Creating /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage/. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> ✔ Creating R/. #> ✔ Writing DESCRIPTION. #> Package: mypackage #> Title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case) #> Version: 0.0.0.9000 #> Authors@R (parsed): #>     * First Last <first.last@example.com> [aut, cre] #> Description: What the package does (one paragraph). #> License: `use_mit_license()`, `use_gpl3_license()` or friends to #>     pick a license #> Encoding: UTF-8 #> Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) #> RoxygenNote: 7.0.0 #> ✔ Writing NAMESPACE. #> ✔ Writing mypackage.Rproj. #> ✔ Adding "^mypackage\\.Rproj$" to .Rbuildignore. #> ✔ Adding ".Rproj.user" to .gitignore. #> ✔ Adding "^\\.Rproj\\.user$" to .Rbuildignore. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/home/runner/work/pkgmkr/pkgmkr". #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> Setting up DESCRIPTION file... #> Creating README.md... #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> ✔ Writing README.md. #> ☐ Update README.md to include installation instructions. #> ✔ Setting active project to "/tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage". #> Setting up MIT license... #> ✔ Adding "MIT + file LICENSE" to License. #> ✔ Writing LICENSE. #> ✔ Writing LICENSE.md. #> ✔ Adding "^LICENSE\\.md$" to .Rbuildignore. #>  #> ============================================================ #> SUCCESS! Package 'mypackage' created at: #> /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/mypackage #> ============================================================ #> ✔ Setting active project to "/home/runner/work/pkgmkr/pkgmkr". unlink(file.path(tempdir(), "mypackage"), recursive = TRUE) unlink(config_path)

title: Write Package Configuration — write_config
text: Write package configuration data YAML file later use mk_pkg_from_config.

title: Write Package Configuration — write_config
text: path character. Path configuration file written. Parent directories created exist. config_data list. configuration data write. Must named list containing package configuration parameters.

title: Write Package Configuration — write_config
text: Invisibly returns TRUE success.

title: Write Package Configuration — write_config
text: function creates YAML configuration file can used create packages consistent settings. parent directory exist, created automatically.

title: Write Package Configuration — write_config
code: # Create a configuration and write it to a temp file config <- list(   pkg_name = "mypackage",   first_name = "Jane",   last_name = "Doe",   email = "jane@example.com",   readme_md = TRUE,   git = FALSE,   license = "MIT" ) config_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".yml") write_config(config_path, config) #> Configuration written successfully to: /tmp/RtmpyZEKzv/file1b66fcfc9a7.yml  # Verify the file was created import_config(config_path, "yaml") #> $pkg_name #> [1] "mypackage" #>  #> $first_name #> [1] "Jane" #>  #> $last_name #> [1] "Doe" #>  #> $email #> [1] "jane@example.com" #>  #> $readme_md #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $git #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $license #> [1] "MIT" #>  unlink(config_path)

title: pkgmkr 0.2.0
text: Maintenance release focused package infrastructure, documentation, site publishing.

title: pkgmkr 0.2.0
text: Added GitHub Actions workflows R CMD check pkgdown deployment. Moved contributor guide .github/CONTRIBUTING.md GitHub can use directly keeping built package contents. Ignored assistant-specific local metadata removed unused local air.toml file repository.

title: pkgmkr 0.2.0
text: Refreshed pkgdown brand configuration removed older custom SCSS override favor new brand-driven theme setup. Rewrote getting-started article better explain one-call config-file workflows. Rebuilt pkgdown site publish updated article, contributing guide, changelog pages.

title: pkgmkr 0.1.0
text: Initial release pkgmkr.

title: pkgmkr 0.1.0
text: Added mk_pkg() create R package single function call, including package structure, metadata, licensing, README creation, optional Git setup, optional pkgdown setup. Added support MIT GPL-3 license setup. Added optional package-name availability checks available package. Added mk_pkg_from_config() creating packages YAML JSON configuration files. Added import_config() write_config() helpers reading writing reusable package-creation configuration files. Added optional author email handling generated package metadata.

title: pkgmkr 0.1.0
text: Added input validation package names, author names, email addresses, license choices, configuration files, existing output directories. Improved error handling around package creation steps optional setup failures produce warnings possible. Added tests package creation, configuration import/export, validation failures, licensing, author-name parsing.

title: pkgmkr 0.1.0
text: Expanded README examples package documentation. Added roxygen documentation regenerated manual pages exported functions. Added pkgdown site configuration, article, theme overrides. Updated package metadata DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, related development files.
